translations.text: So the fate of them both is that they are both in the fire, to remain in it forever; and this is the proper punishment for the unjust.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Their end is, both are in the Fire, there dwelling forever; that is the recompense of the evildoers.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Wherefore the end of the twain will be that they will be in the Fire, as abiders there in; that is the meed of the wrong-doers.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: So the end of both will be that they will be in the Fire, abiding therein. Such is the recompense of the Zalimun (i.e. polytheists, wrong-doers, disbelievers in Allah and in His Oneness, etc.).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: The ultimate end for both of them is the Fire, where they will dwell forever. Such is the requital for the wrongdoers.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: In the end both will be in the Fire, and will abide in it. That is the recompense of the wrong-doers.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: So, the end of both will be that they will be in the Fire, abiding therein. Such is the recompense of the wrongdoers.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And the consequence for both will be that they are in the Fire, therein abiding. Such is the reward of evil-doers.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: So the fate of both is that they will be in the Fire, to remain in it [forever]. Such is the requital of the wrongdoers.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Their end shall be that they live for ever in the Fire. That is the recompense of the harmdoers.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: So the outcome for both of them is that they will be in the Fire, abiding eternally therein. And that is the recompense of the wrong-doers.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: The fate of both of them will be hell fire wherein they will live forever. Thus will be the recompense for the unjust.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Therefore the end of both of them is that they are both in the fire to abide therein, and that is the reward of the unjust.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Thus, in the end, both will find themselves in the Fire, therein to abide: that is the reward of evil-doers.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: The end of both will be that they will go into the Fire, dwelling therein for ever. Such is the reward of the wrong-doers.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Fakana AAaqibatahuma annahuma fee alnnari khalidayni feeha wathalika jazao alththalimeena

18.arabic: يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ ٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَلْتَنظُرْ نَفْسٌ مَّا قَدَّمَتْ لِغَدٍ ۖ وَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ خَبِيرٌۢ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: O you who believe, be fearful of God. Let each soul consider what it has sent (of good deeds) in advance for the morrow, and fear God. Surely God is aware of what you do.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: O People who Believe! Fear Allah, and every soul must see what it has sent ahead for tomorrow; and fear Allah; indeed Allah is Aware of your deeds.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: O believers, fear God. Let every soul consider what it has forwarded for the morrow. And fear God; God is aware of the things you do.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: O ye who believe! fear Allah, and let every soul look to that which it sendeth forward for the morrow. And fear Allah; veril Allah is Aware of that which ye work.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: O you who believe! Fear Allah and keep your duty to Him. And let every person look to what he has sent forth for the morrow, and fear Allah. Verily, Allah is All-Aware of what you do.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: O you who believe! Fear God, and let every soul consider what it has forwarded for the morrow, and fear God. God is Aware of what you do.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Believers, fear Allah and let every person look to what he sends forward for the morrow. Fear Allah; Allah is well aware of all that you do.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: O you who believe! Have Taqwa of Allah and let every person look to what he has sent forth for tomorrow, and fear Allah. Verily, Allah is All-Aware of what you do.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: O ye who believe! Observe your duty to Allah. And let every soul look to that which it sendeth on before for the morrow. And observe your duty to Allah. Lo! Allah is Informed of what ye do.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: O you who have faith! Be wary of Allah, and let every soul consider what it sends ahead for Tomorrow, and be wary of Allah. Allah is indeed well aware of what you do

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Believers, fear Allah. Let every soul look to what it has forwarded for the future, and fear Allah, for Allah is Aware of the things you do.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: O you who have believed, fear Allah. And let every soul look to what it has put forth for tomorrow - and fear Allah. Indeed, Allah is Acquainted with what you do.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Believers, have fear of God. A soul must see what it has done for the future. Have fear of God for He is All-aware of what you do.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: O you who believe! be careful of (your duty to) Allah, and let every soul consider what it has sent on for the morrow, and be careful of (your duty to) Allah; surely Allah is Aware of what you do.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Believers! Fear God, and let every soul look to what it lays up for the future. Fear God: God is aware of what you do.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: O ye who believe! Fear Allah, and let every soul look to what (provision) He has sent forth for the morrow. Yea, fear Allah: for Allah is well-acquainted with (all) that ye do.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Ya ayyuha allatheena amanoo ittaqoo Allaha waltanthur nafsun ma qaddamat lighadin waittaqoo Allaha inna Allaha khabeerun bima taAAmaloona

19.arabic: وَلَا تَكُونُوا۟ كَٱلَّذِينَ نَسُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ فَأَنسَىٰهُمْ أَنفُسَهُمْ ۚ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْفَـٰسِقُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: And be not like those who have forgotten God, so that God has made them forget themselves. Such are the reprobates.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And do not be like those who forgot Allah – He therefore put them into hardship making them forget themselves; it is they who are the sinners.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Be not as those who forgot God, and so He caused them to forget their souls; those -- they are the ungodly.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And be not as those who forgot Allah, wherefore He caused them to forget their own souls. Those! they are the transgressors.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And be not like those who forgot Allah (i.e. became disobedient to Allah) and He caused them to forget their ownselves, (let them to forget to do righteous deeds). Those are the Fasiqun (rebellious, disobedient to Allah).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And do not be like those who forgot God, so He made them forget themselves. These are the sinners.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And be not like those who forgot Allah and so He made them oblivious of themselves. They are the wicked ones.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And be not like those who forgot Allah, and He caused them to forget themselves. Those are the rebellious.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And be not ye as those who forgot Allah, therefor He caused them to forget their souls. Such are the evil-doers.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Do not be like those who forget Allah, so He makes them forget their own souls. It is they who are the transgressors.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Do not be like those who have forgotten Allah so that He has caused them to forget their souls. Those, they are the evildoers.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And be not like those who forgot Allah, so He made them forget themselves. Those are the defiantly disobedient.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Do not be like those who forget about God. He will make them forget themselves. These are the sinful people.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And be not like those who forsook Allah, so He made them forsake their own souls: these it is that are the transgressors.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Do not be like those who forgot God, so that He caused them to forget their own souls [their own true interests]. It is they who are the rebellious ones.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And be ye not like those who forgot Allah; and He made them forget their own souls! Such are the rebellious transgressors!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wala takoonoo kaallatheena nasoo Allaha faansahum anfusahum olaika humu alfasiqoona

20.arabic: لَا يَسْتَوِىٓ أَصْحَـٰبُ ٱلنَّارِ وَأَصْحَـٰبُ ٱلْجَنَّةِ ۚ أَصْحَـٰبُ ٱلْجَنَّةِ هُمُ ٱلْفَآئِزُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Alike are not the inmates of Hell and the residents of Paradise. The men of Paradise will be felicitous.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: The People of hell and the People of Paradise are not equal; it is only the People of Paradise who have succeeded.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Not equal are the inhabitants of the Fire and the inhabitants of Paradise. The inhabitants of Paradise -- they are the triumphant.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Not equal are the fellows of the Fire and the fellows of the Garden. Fellows of the Garden! they are the achievers!

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Not equal are the dwellers of the Fire and the dwellers of the Paradise. It is the dwellers of Paradise that will be successful.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Not equal are the inhabitants of the Fire and the inhabitants of Paradise. It is the inhabitants of Paradise who are the winners.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Those destined for the Fire and those destined for Paradise cannot be alike. Verily it is those destined for Paradise who shall triumph.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Not equal are the dwellers of the Fire and the dwellers of the Paradise. It is the dwellers of Paradise that will be successful.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Not equal are the owners of the Fire and the owners of the Garden. The owners of the Garden, they are the victorious.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Not equal are the inmates of the Fire and the inhabitants of paradise. It is the inhabitants of paradise who are the successful ones.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: The inhabitants of the Fire and the inhabitants of Paradise are not equal. The inhabitants of Paradise shall be triumphant.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Not equal are the companions of the Fire and the companions of Paradise. The companions of Paradise - they are the attainers [of success].

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: The Companions of the Fire and the Companions of the Garden are not equal. The Companions of the Garden will achieve felicity.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Not alike are the inmates of the fire and the dwellers of the garden: the dwellers of the garden are they that are the achievers.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: The people of the Fire and the people of Paradise are not equal. The people of Paradise are the victorious ones.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Not equal are the Companions of the Fire and the Companions of the Garden: it is the Companions of the Garden, that will achieve Felicity.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: La yastawee ashabu alnnari waashabu aljannati ashabu aljannati humu alfaizoona

21.arabic: لَوْ أَنزَلْنَا هَـٰذَا ٱلْقُرْءَانَ عَلَىٰ جَبَلٍ لَّرَأَيْتَهُۥ خَـٰشِعًا مُّتَصَدِّعًا مِّنْ خَشْيَةِ ٱللَّهِ ۚ وَتِلْكَ ٱلْأَمْثَـٰلُ نَضْرِبُهَا لِلنَّاسِ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَتَفَكَّرُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: If We had sent down this Qur'an to a mountain you would have seen it turn desolate and split into two for fear of God. We offer these examples to men that they may think and reflect.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Had We sent down this Qur’an upon a mountain, you would have then surely seen it bowed down, blown to bits by the fear of Allah; and We illustrate such examples for people, for them to ponder.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: If We had sent down this Koran upon a mountain, thou wouldst have seen it humbled, split asunder out of the fear of God. And those similitudes -- We strike them for men; haply they will reflect.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Had We sent down this Qur'an upon a mountain, thou wouldst surely have seen it humbling itself and cleaving in sunder for fear of Allah. Such similitudes We propound Unto mankind that haply they may reflect.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Had We sent down this Quran on a mountain, you would surely have seen it humbling itself and rending asunder by the fear of Allah. Such are the parables which We put forward to mankind that they may reflect.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Had We sent this Quran down on a mountain, you would have seen it trembling, crumbling in awe of God. These parables We cite for the people, so that they may reflect.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Had We sent down this Qur'an upon a mountain you would indeed have seen it humbling itself and breaking asunder out of fear of Allah. We propound such parables to people that they may reflect.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Had We sent down this Qur'an on a mountain, you would surely have seen it humbling itself and rent asunder by the fear of Allah. Such are the parables which We put forward to mankind that they may reflect.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: If We had caused this Qur'an to descend upon a mountain, thou (O Muhammad) verily hadst seen it humbled, rent asunder by the fear of Allah. Such similitudes coin We for mankind that haply they may reflect.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Had We sent down this Quran upon a mountain, you would have seen it humbled [and] go to pieces with the fear of Allah. We draw such comparisons for mankind, so that they may reflect.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Had We sent down this Koran upon a mountain, you would have seen it humble itself and split asunder for fear of Allah. Such are the parables we strike for people so that they will reflect.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: If We had sent down this Qur'an upon a mountain, you would have seen it humbled and coming apart from fear of Allah. And these examples We present to the people that perhaps they will give thought.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: The people of Paradise and hell are not alike; the people of Paradise are the successful ones. Had We sent down this Quran on a mountain, you would have seen it humbled and rent asunder for fear of God. These are parables which We tell to people so that perhaps they will think.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Had We sent down this Quran on a mountain, you would certainly have seen it falling down, splitting asunder because of the fear of Allah, and We set forth these parables to men that they may reflect.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Had We sent down this Quran on a mountain, you would certainly have seen it falling down and splitting asunder, because of the fear of God. We set forth these parables to men so that they may reflect.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Had We sent down this Qur'an on a mountain, verily, thou wouldst have seen it humble itself and cleave asunder for fear of Allah. Such are the similitudes which We propound to men, that they may reflect.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Law anzalna hatha alqurana AAala jabalin laraaytahu khashiAAan mutasaddiAAan min khashyati Allahi watilka alamthalu nadribuha lilnnasi laAAallahum yatafakkaroona

22.arabic: هُوَ ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِى لَآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ۖ عَـٰلِمُ ٱلْغَيْبِ وَٱلشَّهَـٰدَةِ ۖ هُوَ ٱلرَّحْمَـٰنُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: He is God; there is no god but He, the knower of the unknown and the known. He is the benevolent, ever-merciful.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: It is Allah, except Whom there is no God; the Knowing of all – the hidden and the evident; He only is the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: He is God; there is no god but He. He is the knower of the Unseen and the Visible; He is the All-merciful, the All-compassionate.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: He is Allah, there is no God but He; Knower of the unseen and the seen. He is the Compassionate, the Merciful.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: He is Allah, than Whom there is La ilaha illa Huwa (none has the right to be worshipped but He) the All-Knower of the unseen and the seen (open). He is the Most Beneficent, the Most Merciful.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: He is God. There is no god but He, the Knower of secrets and declarations. He is the Compassionate, the Merciful.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: He is Allah: there is no god but He; the Knower of the unseen and the manifest, He is the Most Merciful, the Most Compassionate.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: He is Allah, beside Whom La ilaha illa Huwa, the All-Knower of the unseen and the seen. He is the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: He is Allah, than Whom there is no other Allah, the Knower of the Invisible and the Visible. He is the Beneficent, Merciful.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: He is Allah—there is no god except Him—Knower of the sensible and the Unseen, He is the All-beneficent, the All-merciful.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: He is Allah, there is no god except He. He knows the Unseen and the Visible. He is the Merciful, the Most Merciful.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: He is Allah, other than whom there is no deity, Knower of the unseen and the witnessed. He is the Entirely Merciful, the Especially Merciful.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: He is God, the only Lord, Who knows the unseen and the seen. He is the Beneficent and All-merciful One.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: He is Allah besides Whom there is no god; the Knower of the unseen and the seen; He is the Beneficent, the Merciful

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: He is God: there is no deity save Him. He knows the unseen and the visible. He is the Compassionate, the Merciful.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Allah is He, than Whom there is no other god;- Who knows (all things) both secret and open; He, Most Gracious, Most Merciful.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Huwa Allahu allathee la ilaha illa huwa AAalimu alghaybi waalshshahadati huwa alrrahmanu alrraheemu

23.arabic: هُوَ ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِى لَآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ٱلْمَلِكُ ٱلْقُدُّوسُ ٱلسَّلَـٰمُ ٱلْمُؤْمِنُ ٱلْمُهَيْمِنُ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلْجَبَّارُ ٱلْمُتَكَبِّرُ ۚ سُبْحَـٰنَ ٱللَّهِ عَمَّا يُشْرِكُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: He is God; there is no god but He, the King, the Holy, the Preserver, Protector, Guardian, the Strong, the Powerful, Omnipotent. Far too exalted is God for what they associate with Him.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: It is Allah, except Whom there is no God; the King, the Pure, the Giver of Peace, the Bestower of Safety, the Protector, the Most Honourable, the Compeller, the Proud; Purity is to Allah from all what they ascribe as partners (to Him)!

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: He is God; there is no god but He. He is the King, the All-holy, the All-peaceable, the All-faithful, the All-preserver, the All-mighty, the All-compeller, the All-sublime. Glory be to God, above that they associate!

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: He is Allah, there is no God but He, the Sovereign, the Holy, the Author of Safety, the Giver of Peace, the Protector, the Mighty, the Mender, the Majestic. Hallowed be Allah from all that they associate!

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: He is Allah than Whom there is La ilaha illa Huwa (none has the right to be worshipped but He) the King, the Holy, the One Free from all defects, the Giver of security, the Watcher over His creatures, the All-Mighty, the Compeller, the Supreme. Glory be to Allah! (High is He) above all that they associate as partners with Him.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: He is God; besides Whom there is no god; the Sovereign, the Holy, the Peace-Giver, the Faith-Giver, the Overseer, the Almighty, the Omnipotent, the Overwhelming. Glory be to God, beyond what they associate.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: He is Allah: there is no god but He: the King, the Holy, the All-Peace, the Giver of security, the Overseer, the Most Mighty, the Overpowering, the All-Great. Exalted be He from whatever they associate with Him.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: He is Allah, beside Whom La ilaha illa Huwa, Al-Malik, Al-Quddus, As-Salam, Al-Mu`min, Al-Muhaymin, Al-`Aziz, Al-Jabbar, Al-Mutakabbir. Glory be to Allah! Above all that they associate as partners with Him.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: He is Allah, than Whom there is no other Allah, the Sovereign Lord, the Holy One, Peace, the Keeper of Faith, the Guardian, the Majestic, the Compeller, the Superb. Glorified be Allah from all that they ascribe as partner (unto Him).

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: He is Allah—there is no god except Him—the Sovereign, the All-holy, the All-benign, the Securer, the All-conserver, the All-mighty, the All-compeller, and the All-magnanimous. Clear is Allah of any partners that they may ascribe [to Him]!

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: He is Allah, there is no god except He. He is the King, the Pure, the Peace, the Confirmer, the Watchful, the Almighty, the Compeller, the Sublime. Exalted is Allah, above all that they associate!

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: He is Allah, other than whom there is no deity, the Sovereign, the Pure, the Perfection, the Bestower of Faith, the Overseer, the Exalted in Might, the Compeller, the Superior. Exalted is Allah above whatever they associate with Him.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: He is the only Lord, the King, the Holy, the Peace, the Forgiver, the Watchful Guardian, the Majestic, the Dominant, and the Exalted. God is too exalted to have any partner.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: He is Allah, besides Whom there is no god; the King, the Holy, the Giver of peace, the Granter of security, Guardian over all, the Mighty, the Supreme, the Possessor of every greatness Glory be to Allah from what they set up (with Him).

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: He is God, there is no deity save Him, the Sovereign, the Most Pure, the Source of Peace, the Granter of Security, the Protector, the Mighty, the Subduer, the Supreme, Glory be to God, who is far above what they associate with Him.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Allah is He, than Whom there is no other god;- the Sovereign, the Holy One, the Source of Peace (and Perfection), the Guardian of Faith, the Preserver of Safety, the Exalted in Might, the Irresistible, the Supreme: Glory to Allah! (High is He) above the partners they attribute to Him.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Huwa Allahu allathee la ilaha illa huwa almaliku alquddoosu alssalamu almuminu almuhayminu alAAazeezu aljabbaru almutakabbiru subhana Allahi AAamma yushrikoona

24.arabic: هُوَ ٱللَّهُ ٱلْخَـٰلِقُ ٱلْبَارِئُ ٱلْمُصَوِّرُ ۖ لَهُ ٱلْأَسْمَآءُ ٱلْحُسْنَىٰ ۚ يُسَبِّحُ لَهُۥ مَا فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۖ وَهُوَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلْحَكِيمُ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: He is God, the Creator, the Maker, the Fashioner. His are all the names beautiful. Whatever is in the heavens and the earth sings His praises. He is all-mighty and all-wise.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: It is Allah only, Who is the Creator, the Initiator, the Designer of all – His only are all the good names; all whatever is in the heavens and in the earth proclaims His Purity; and He only is the Most Honourable, the Wise.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: He is God, the Creator, the Maker, the Shaper. To Him belong the Names Most Beautiful. All that is in the heavens and the earth magnifies Him; He is the All-mighty, the All-wise.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: He is Allah, the Creator, the Maker, the Fashioner; His are the excellent names. Him halloweth whatsoever is in the heavens and the earth, and He is the Mighty, the Wise.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: He is Allah, the Creator, the Inventor of all things, the Bestower of forms. To Him belong the Best Names. All that is in the heavens and the earth glorify Him. And He is the All-Mighty, the All-Wise.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: He is God; the Creator, the Maker, the Designer. His are the Most Beautiful Names. Whatever is in the heavens and the earth glorifies Him. He is the Majestic, the Wise.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: He is Allah, the Planner, Executer and Fashioner of creation. His are the names most beautiful. Whatever is in the heavens and the earth extols His Glory. He is the Most Mighty, the Most Wise.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: He is Allah, Al-Khaliq, Al-Bari, Al-Musawwir. To Him belong the Best Names. All that is in the heavens and the earth glorify Him. And He is the Almighty, the All-Wise.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: He is Allah, the Creator, the Shaper out of naught, the Fashioner. His are the most beautiful names. All that is in the heavens and the earth glorifieth Him, and He is the Mighty, the Wise.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: He is Allah, the Creator, the Maker, and the Former. To Him belong the Best Names. Whatever there is in the heavens and the earth glorifies Him and He is the All-mighty, the All-wise.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: He is Allah, the Creator, the Originator, the Shaper. To Him belong the Most Beautiful Names. All that is in the heavens and earth exalt Him. He is theAlmighty, the Wise.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: He is Allah, the Creator, the Inventor, the Fashioner; to Him belong the best names. Whatever is in the heavens and earth is exalting Him. And He is the Exalted in Might, the Wise.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: He is the Creator, the Designer, the Modeler, and to Him belong all virtuous names. All that is in the heavens and the earth glorify Him. He is All-majestic and All-wise.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: He is Allah the Creator, the Maker, the Fashioner; His are the most excellent names; whatever is in the heavens and the earth declares His glory; and He is the Mighty, the Wise.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: He is God -- the Creator, the Originator, the Giver of Form. His are the most excellent names. Everything in the heavens and earth declares His glory. He is the Mighty, the Wise One.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: He is Allah, the Creator, the Evolver, the Bestower of Forms (or Colours). To Him belong the Most Beautiful Names: whatever is in the heavens and on earth, doth declare His Praises and Glory: and He is the Exalted in Might, the Wise.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Huwa Allahu alkhaliqu albario almusawwiru lahu alasmao alhusna yusabbihu lahu ma fee alssamawati waalardi wahuwa alAAazeezu alhakeemu
